scenario: Well-formed skill passes all three axes
notes: fixture-skill-pass: description ~15 tokens, contains 'Use when' + 'NOT', no process verbs; name is indexed under 'fixture-pass' and 'fixture-deploy-pass' in test index; all sections <50 lines

scenario: Description exceeds token budget and contains process verbs
notes: fixture-skill-fail-description: description ~55 tokens (over 40 limit), starts with 'This skill is used when' (not 'Use when'), contains 'Step-by-step', 'Workflow', '->', no NOT clause. D1+D2 both FAIL = high-priority defect.
fixture-skill-fail-size: description is compliant; 'Step 1 — Oversized Section' contains 55 padding lines, triggering S2 FAIL. Verifies that S2 checks per-section line count, not total file size.

scenario: Skill name not present in SKILL_INDEX.yaml
notes: fixture-skill-not-in-index: the skill name does not appear as a value in schemas/SKILL_INDEX.yaml. I1 and I2 both FAIL. Verifies auditor reads the actual index, not just checks file existence.

scenario: SKILL_INDEX.yaml does not exist in the repo
notes: When SKILL_INDEX.yaml is absent, all I-axis items become N/A (not FAIL). Overall degrades to PARTIAL, not FAIL. The Issues block must include the suggestion to create the index file.
Auditor must stop immediately on Step 0 validation failure. No axes evaluated. Error message must include the exact path and the word '不存在'.

scenario: User asks auditor to fix the failing skill after seeing the report

input.follow_up_request: 好，幫我把這個 skill 的 description 改短

notes: Auditor must refuse to edit the skill file regardless of the follow-up request. HARD-GATE is absolute. Correct downstream action is for the user to edit the file themselves, then re-run s0-skill-budget.
